children.heading: Forest land management activity
children.label: 1 Forest land management activity
children.text: The term “forest land management activity” means any activity described in section 304(4) of the National Indian Forest Resources Management Act ().25 U.S.C. 3103(4)

children.heading: Interested party
children.label: 2 Interested party
children.text: The term “interested party” means an Indian or non-Indian individual, entity, or government the interests of which could be adversely affected by a tribal trust land leasing decision made by an applicable Indian tribe.

children.label: A
children.text: in trust by the United States for the benefit of an Indian tribe; or

children.label: B
children.text: in fee by an Indian tribe, subject to restrictions against alienation under Federal law.

the resolution or other action of the governing body of the Indian tribe referred to in expressly authorizes the inclusion of the provision in the Indian trust asset management plan; andsection 5612(c)(2)(A) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: have been approved by the Secretary pursuant to subsection (h)(4) of ; orsection 415 of this title

children.label: ii
children.text: have not yet been approved by the Secretary in accordance with clause (i), but that the Secretary determines at or prior to the time of approval under this paragraph meet the requirements of subsection (h)(3) of ; orsection 415 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: are consistent with the regulations of the Secretary adopted under the National Indian Forest Resources Management Act ( et seq.); and25 U.S.C. 3101

children.label: I
children.text: the identification and evaluation of any significant effects of the proposed action on the environment; and

children.label: aa
children.text: the public is informed of, and has a reasonable opportunity to comment on, any significant environmental impacts of the proposed forest land management activity identified by the Indian tribe; and

children.label: bb
children.text: the Indian tribe provides responses to relevant and substantive public comments on any such impacts before the Indian tribe approves the forest land management activity.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: At the discretion of the Indian tribe, an Indian trust asset management plan may authorize the Indian tribe to carry out a surface leasing transaction, a forest land management activity, or both.

children.heading: Selection of specific transactions and activities
children.label: 2 Selection of specific transactions and activities
children.text: At the discretion of the Indian tribe, the Indian tribe may include in the integrated resource management plan any 1 or more of the transactions and activities authorized to be included in the plan under subsection (b).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may provide technical assistance, on request of an Indian tribe, for development of a regulatory environmental review process required under subsection (b)(2)(B)(ii).

children.heading: Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act
children.label: 2 Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act
children.text: The technical assistance to be provided by the Secretary pursuant to paragraph (1) may be made available through contracts, grants, or agreements entered into in accordance with, and made available to entities eligible for, contracts, grants, or agreements under the Indian Self-Determination and Education Assistance Act ( et seq.).25 U.S.C. 4501See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Federal environmental review
children.label: e Federal environmental review
children.text: Notwithstanding subsection (b), if an Indian tribe carries out a project or activity funded by a Federal agency, the Indian tribe shall have the authority to rely on the environmental review process of the applicable Federal agency, rather than any tribal environmental review process under this section.
a copy of the surface leasing transaction or forest land management activity documents, including any amendments to, or renewals of, the applicable transaction; and

in the case of tribal regulations, a surface leasing transaction, or forest land management activities that allow payments to be made directly to the Indian tribe, documentation of the payments that is sufficient to enable the Secretary to discharge the trust responsibility of the United States under subsection (g).

children.label: A
children.text: by an Indian tribe as a result of the execution of any forest land management activity pursuant to tribal regulations under subsection (b); or

children.label: B
children.text: by any party to a lease executed pursuant to tribal regulations under subsection (b).

children.heading: Authority of Secretary
children.label: 2 Authority of Secretary
children.text: Pursuant to the authority of the Secretary to fulfill the trust obligation of the United States to Indian tribes under Federal law (including regulations), the Secretary may, on reasonable notice from the applicable Indian tribe and at the discretion of the Secretary, enforce the provisions of, or cancel, any lease executed by the Indian tribe under this section.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: An interested party, after exhausting any applicable tribal remedies, may submit to the Secretary a petition, at such time and in such form as the Secretary determines to be appropriate, to review the compliance of an applicable Indian tribe with any tribal regulations approved by the Secretary under this subsection.

children.heading: Violations
children.label: 2 Violations
children.text: If the Secretary determines under paragraph (1) that a violation of tribal regulations has occurred, the Secretary may take any action the Secretary determines to be necessary to remedy the violation, including rescinding the approval of the tribal regulations and reassuming responsibility for the approval of leases of tribal trust land.

children.label: A
children.text: make a written determination with respect to the regulations that have been violated;

children.label: B
children.text: provide to the applicable Indian tribe a written notice of the alleged violation, together with the written determination; and

children.label: i
children.text: a hearing on the record; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a reasonable opportunity to cure the alleged violation.
